Limited Time Summon.text:
**Limited Time Summon**
The limited time summon event is same for both red and blue limited time summon card. These summon card can be saved for future use.

7 day long event where you get to buy summon card.

For blue buy and saved to convert blue to red ticket.

For red buy the red ticket and depends on what the 7 day rerun character are, you can decide whether you want to save or use it.

For red limited time summon event, is 7 day long and 3 character is available to choose, this is a rerun not a new release.

**Update: Blazing Unit also have a 7 day long rerun event same concept as blue and red ticket event, you get to purchase Blazing Ring 2500 diamonds for 5 Ring.**

Duck Hunt.text:
**Duck Hunt**
Buy bullet for 2500 diamond for 5 bullet, save your bullets.

If you want to min max rewards, a minimum of 200-220 bullet will ensure you in exchanging one time wheel from shop and hitting the ranking rewards of use bullet 200 times.

Bullet can be earned by using diamonds to purchase, cash & Holy Grail War.

Charge Gift.text:
**Charge Gift**
Spend 2500 diamond to buy 5 charge, can be bought up to 5 times.

One full run from 0 to fully charged orange is about 120 charge (minimum).

Best is to save more, with the given recent update if you can saved 350 charge before using it would be the best as you will be earning new mythic card from the ranking.

Companion Summon.text:
**Companion Event**
2500 diamond for 5 companion summon card, buy and save it, only use it if you have enough to hit the 60 pity mark.

If your planning to rely on the RNG from the normal pet summon. All I can say is that the % of you getting an Awk pet from it is lower than you expect. Is relatively hard to get awaken pet from the normal pet summon.

Thought event you have a pity of 60 in getting 1 awaken pet, while you summon for it, there's a chance for you to get red blessing stone for your blessing which is also extremely important.

Note buy only if you have excess diamonds saved up, priorities in limited summon/duck hunt/charge event before considering companion summon.

Assigned Swap.text:
**Assigned Swap**
If you ever consider in getting every new release character to like a m9 or e1. Do take note of swap event when it comes.

Let's say you had like 3/4 unit staying at m9 that you don't use. You can take advantage of the swap event to swap in all those unit into one strong LTD unit. Of course is not for free.

The weekly pass of 29.99 you will get 60 swap gem to use. Depending on what star you currently at the higher the star will used more gem in swapping.

Shop selling $99.99 for 64 gem, so take advantage of the $29.99 weekly pack for 60 gem, the swap gem can be saved for future use.

Shrine Wish.text:
**Shrine Wish**
## Shrine Wish Ticket cannot be saved, if your a F2P or Mid-Spender this event is a waste of diamond unless you have a lot of diamond spare.
# Shrine ticket will be convert to diamond after event end if you did not used it

Buy Shrine Ticket for diamonds or earn some from Cross Server Challenge. Get you pass the first level of prize pool. Starting from 2nd Level Prize Pool will be all $$$ only. Whale gets resources that they needed from here while farming Title on ranking system.

The cross server challenge have ranking depending on who deal the most damage to all 3 bosses, as long as your within the % reward you will get it, doesn't matter whose 1st/2nd/3rd.

Sea Treasure Hunt.text:
**Sea Treasure Hunt**
Main event Resist the Tide
- 200 Wave everyday, 7 day total 1400 Wave
- Fastest to clear the 1400th Wave in Day 7 when reset get 1st

Purple Gold
- Events Currency
- Get from Resist the Tide & buying from shop
- Mainly used to exchange material from Treasure Hunt

Treasure Hunt
- Spend Purple Gold to get ranking for extra rewards
- What to Exchange?
> Lucky Chest
> Soul Jade Origin
> Optional Fated Soul Jade Pack
> Optional Legendary Card
> Optional Mythic Card
> King Cloth Pack
> Advanced Ascension Pack
> 
> If you spend and reach LVL 4 shop
> Mark of the God
> Reformation Time Wheel
## The above are just recommendation on what is more valuable to exchange, you can always get what your lacking.

Ninja Tool.text:
**Ninja Tools**
## Ninja Scroll can be saved

- Scrolls are 3000 diamond for 5

Minimum scrolls before start using is **80**
Why 80?
Ninja tools are 40/copy with 80 scrolls you can get 2 ninja tools and 1 Ninja Tools Chest. Which bring the total to 3 Ninja Tools/80scroll

Daily Shop, Item Shop , Medal Hero & Clo.text:
**Daily Shop**
Select all gold item - when you have excess gold
Low gold priority:
Challenge voucher & Helm

**Item Shop**
Pretty much 25 diamonds for the advance summon sticker.

**Medal Hero & Cloth**
Medal Hero is not recommended  ❌ 

Medal Cloth is where you truly spend your medal on  ✅
Buy sets for your main dps unit > support.

If you have extra diamonds you can buy the 750 special card, not really recommended if your low on diamonds, buying event item comes in priority.

Other item buy at your own needs.

Points Shop.text:
**Crusade Shop**
For early on you can buy Akame Shards.

Treasure Box Key should be bought out every week.

Divine Relic once u hit mid game when you don't need any of the 3 fraction unit shards.

**Alliance Shop**
Focus in buying Rune chest, letter of friendship, 3star gear and 4star gear - early on

Eventually when you start getting merge and alliance having a lot of SJW player you can pretty much clear out the whole shop weekly and have extra coins.

**Treasure Hunt Shop**
Refresh for Sand Dust.

Other items buy according to your needs.

**Maze Shop**
Only buy Reformation Time Wheel shards.

Purchase Limited Shop.text:
**Tower of Infinity**
Buy according to your needs when you have excess diamonds.

**PVP Purchase Limit**
Buy according to your needs when you have excess diamonds.

**Grand Voyager**
Buy according to your needs when you have excess diamonds.

**Hueco Mundo Trials**
Buy according to your needs when you have excess diamonds.

PvP shop etc..text:
**Arena Shop**
Buy out Special Card, Millennium Key & Golden Card Pack Ticket.

Other items like Goku Shards, buy according to your needs when you have extra.

**Cross Server Shop**
1) Energy Crystal
2) Fated Soul Jade Pack
3) Kekkei Genkai
4) Legendary Soul
5) Reversion Token

**Budo Shop**
1) Soul Jade Origin
2) Any of the 3 Tokens
3) Sand Dust

**Demon Shop**
Choices are really depending on you, nothing is wrong here, buy according to the material that you are lacking.

❌ Of course avoid buying things like coins, golden card pack ticket & fraction hero ❌

**Hall of Hero - Heroic Spirit Shop**
Choices are really depending on you, nothing is wrong here, buy according to the material that you are lacking. If possible prioritize buying out Energy Crystal & Sand Dust Chest first, before start buying TG materials.

❌ Essence of Revelation & 3-Fraction Legendary Clone Pack is not really recommended in buying ❌

Infinite Train, Soul Jade War, Forest of.text:
**Infinite Train - Cloth Exchange**
Exchange clothes for your main team, Taurus, Sagi, Gemini, Virgo, Scorpio & etc.

**Soul Jade War - Dimension Shop**
Golden Key will be your main target weekly.

Scorpio Set buy it for late game use.

Special Card buy out.

Hero Shard buy if you're lacking and need to do Hero Assembly.

**Forest of Void - Loop Coin Shop**
1) Spiritual Crystalization (Pet Crystal)
2) Energy Crystal & Super Dragon Ball
3) Legendary Blessing Stone (Buy until all your 4 class blessing are either full orange or full red, stop buying once everything is full)
4) Sand Dust (Red/Purple/Blue, depending on which your lacking)

**Pursuit Rogue - Pursuit Shop**
Buy according to your needs. Nothing is wrong here, buy what you're lacking. 

This is a late game shop for those DPS/Bruiser/Support that is TG and wanting to start building their Ninja Weapon.

Full Tank.text:
__FULL TANK__

A full tank is a frontline unit that:

• absorbs large amounts of damage
• has very high HP, defense, or damage reduction
• protects the team by drawing enemy attacks

Its objective is:

Maintain frontline stability by absorbing enemy damage and creating space for damage dealers and supports to perform effectively.

DPS Assassin.text:
__DPS ASSASSIN__

A DPS assassin is an assassin-type unit that:

• focuses on dealing high damage
• targets the enemy backline
• specializes in eliminating fragile damage dealers or supports

Its objective is:

Prioritize eliminating high-value targets quickly to weaken the enemy team and create a decisive advantage during the fight.

Off Tank.text:
__OFF-TANK__

An off-tank is a frontline unit that:

• combines durability with utility or damage
• can absorb a moderate amount of damage
• supports the frontline while contributing with control, buffs, debuffs, or extra damage

Its objective is:

Assist the main tank in maintaining frontline stability while applying additional pressure and utility during the fight.

DPS Mage.text:
__DPS MAGE__

A DPS mage is a mage-type unit that:

• deals consistent magic damage
• relies on abilities to damage enemies over time
• can pressure multiple targets with spells

Its objective is:

Apply sustained magical damage to weaken the enemy team and support allies by constantly pressuring enemy units throughout the fight.

DoT Support.text:
__DOT SUPPORT__

A DoT support is a support-type unit that:

• applies damage over time (DoT) effects
• weakens enemies through continuous damage or debuffs
• supports the team by pressuring opponents over time

Its objective is:

Gradually wear down the enemy team with persistent damage effects while supporting allies and disrupting the enemy’s ability to sustain the fight.

Heavy Debuffer.text:
__HEAVY DEBUFFER__

A heavy debuffer is a support-type unit that:

• applies multiple debuffs to enemies
• weakens enemy damage, defense, or effectiveness
• disrupts enemy strategies during the fight

Its objective is:

Reduce the enemy team’s combat effectiveness by applying strong debuffs, making it easier for allies to survive and deal damage.

Buffer.text:
__BUFFER__

A buffer is a support-type unit that:

• applies buffs to allies
• increases stats such as ATK, DEF, or speed
• improves the overall effectiveness of the team

Its objective is:

Strengthen allies by enhancing their abilities and combat performance, allowing the team to deal more damage and survive longer in battle.

Healer.text:
__HEALER__

A healer is a support-type unit that:

• restores HP to allies
• keeps the team alive during battles
• provides sustain through healing abilities

Its objective is:

Maintain the team’s survivability by continuously restoring health and preventing allies from being eliminated during the fight.

Assist.text:
__ASSIST__

An assist is a support-type unit that:

• without being on field, it enhances allies through utility effects
• provides indirect support such as energy gain, buffs, or synergy effects
• contributes to the team without being a primary damage dealer
• replacing your death unit to continue your team synergy

Its objective is:

Support the team by improving overall performance through utility, enabling allies to perform better and maintain control of the fight.

Bursty Mage.text:
__BURSTY MAGE__

A bursty mage is a mage-type unit that:

• deals extremely high magic burst damage
• relies on powerful skills or ultimate abilities
• can eliminate targets quickly with spell combos

Its objective is:

Prioritize eliminating fragile enemy units or dealing massive damage to multiple targets early in the fight to gain a decisive advantage.

Bursty Assassin.text:
__BURSTY ASSASSIN__

A bursty assassin is an assassin-type unit that:

• deals very high burst damage
• eliminates a target very quickly
• often in the first seconds of the fight

Its objective is:

Prioritize neutralizing the opponent’s main damage sources or eliminating fragile supports early to prevent the fight from shifting in their favor

Aries ♈️.text:
## __♈ Aries Set__

Set Bonus:
• 2 pieces: ATK +6%
• 4 pieces: ATK +9%

__Set Function__

This is a purely offensive set.

It increases the base damage of heroes because:
• it increases total ATK
• which increases skill damage, basic attacks, and burst damage

It is the classic “sharp sword” set: simple and direct.

__Best Heroes for This Set__

Works best on:

Pure DPS
Heroes that scale directly with ATK.
Typical examples:
• Single target DPS
• Burst damage heroes
• Main team carries
• Healers 
• Buffer

Not ideal for:
• Tanks
• Supports (That doesn't required ATK scaling)


because these usually scale with HP / DEF / Energy / Speed.

__When to Use It__

Early game: ⭐⭐⭐⭐
Very strong because it is easy to obtain.

Mid game: ⭐⭐⭐
Still useful on secondary DPS.

Late game: ⭐⭐
Usually replaced by sets with more complex effects (crit, skill damage, energy, etc.).

__Set Logic__

This set is designed as a:

__Universal raw damage set__

If you are unsure what to equip on a DPS, Aries almost always works.

It is the set the game gives you to stabilize your damage output in the early stages.

Useful tip:
The Aries set is often used as a temporary set while you are farming better gear sets.

Taurus ♉️.text:
## __♉ Taurus Set__

Set Bonus

2 pieces
• ATK +12%
4 pieces — Skill: Aldebaran

Very unique effect:

1️⃣ When the battle starts
• Gain 24% Damage Reduction

2️⃣ After 10 seconds
• Every 5 seconds:
• Lose 6% Damage Reduction
• Gain 6% Damage Bonus

3️⃣ After 25 seconds
• The effect becomes:
• +24% Damage Bonus permanently until the end of the fight

This effect cannot be dispelled.

__How It Actually Works__

This set is designed as a transformation from defense to offense.

At the beginning:
Very tanky

Then slowly:
It becomes more and more offensive.

__Best Heroes for This Set__

This set is perfect for heroes that need to survive the early part of the fight and deal damage later.

Ideal for:

• Bruisers
(tanks that deal damage)
• Frontline DPS
• Carries that need time to charge their skills

__Not Ideal For__

• Fast burst DPS
(because the bonus comes late)
• Support / healers
• Very short fights

__Where It Is Strong__

• Long PvP fights
• Boss fights
• Game modes where battles last a long time

If the fight lasts less than 10–15 seconds, this set loses a lot of value.

__Small suggestion__
Helps weaker units survive the opponent’s initial burst, giving them a better chance to turn the fight around

Gemini ♊️.text:
## __♊ Gemini Set__

Set Bonus

2 pieces
• ATK +12%

4 pieces — Skill: Pollux

When you apply a control effect, the following happens:

The target loses 300 additional Rage

⚠️ Limitation:
• The same target can trigger this effect only once every 10 seconds


__Which effects count as Control__

The game considers these as control effects:
• Stun
• Immobilize
• Taunt
• Binding
• Silence
• Deterrence
• Blockade

So any hero that applies these effects can activate the set.

__What losing 300 Rage means__

Rage is essentially the energy used to cast the Ultimate.

So this set is used to:

❌ delay enemy ultimates

If an enemy:
• gets controlled
• loses 300 Rage

it becomes much harder for them to cast their skill.

__Real function of the set__

This is an anti-ultimate set.

It helps to:
• slow down skill rotations
• break enemy combos
• control the pace of the fight

It is more strategic than many other sets.

__Best heroes for this set__

It only works on heroes with control abilities.
__Ideal for__

Controllers Heroes that apply:
• stun
• silence (Can be trigger by soul jade)
• immobilize
• taunt

__Not useful for__

❌ Pure DPS
❌ Healers
❌ Supports without control

Because they would never trigger the set effect

Cancer ♋️.text:
## __♋ Cancer Set__

__Set Bonus__

2 pieces
• CRIT Rate +6%

4 pieces
• CRIT Rate +9% total

So the full set gives +15% critical chance.

__Set Function__

This set is designed to increase crit consistency.
It does not increase damage directly, but it increases the chance that attacks:
become critical hits.
In games where critical hits multiply damage, this can be very valuable.

__Best Heroes for This Set__

It works best on heroes that:
• perform many attacks
• have abilities that can crit
• scale well with critical damage

__Ideal for__

• Burst DPS
• Assassin / single target
• Main carries

__Not Ideal For__

• Tanks
• Supports
• Controllers

because these roles do not benefit much from critical hits.

__Why It Is Used__

If a DPS has for example:
• 30% base crit

with the Cancer set it becomes:
45% crit

This greatly increases the probability of chaining critical hits.
It is a solid but simple set.

__It is usually used__
• in early game
• on DPS that do not yet have much crit.

__Useful Tip__

Many players use Cancer when:
• their hero cannot reach the crit cap
• or they are waiting for better sets.

__Small suggestion__

There is also a trick to use it on sub dmg dealer (mage or assasin with crit soul jade and NP Lotus)

Leo ♌️.text:
## __♌ Leo Set__

__Set Bonus__

2 pieces
• CRIT Rate +12%

This alone is already very strong because it is higher than the Cancer set.

4 pieces — Skill: Regulus

When the character uses:
• Technique
• Ultimate

a special effect is triggered.

__Effect__

Deals bonus damage equal to 6% of the target’s maximum HP.

__Maximum limit__
• cannot exceed 240% of your hero’s ATK

__Other important details__

• Ignores enemy DEF
• 5-second cooldown

__How It Actually Works__

This is percentage damage based on the enemy’s HP.

So:
• the more HP the target has
• the more damage you deal

For this reason it is very strong against:

• Tanks
• Bosses
• Enemies with very high HP

__Why It Is Strong__

The damage:

• ignores defense
• scales with enemy HP
• activates with important skills

This greatly increases the burst potential of ultimates.

__Best Heroes for This Set__

Perfect for heroes that:
• use ultimates frequently
• deal burst damage
• have area skills

__Ideal for__

• Main DPS
• Burst heroes
• AoE damage dealers

__Not Ideal For__

• Tanks
• Supports
• Controllers without damage

__Important Note__

The reason Leo is strong is this:

Many tanks have extremely high HP but also very high defense.

__The Leo set__

• ignores defense
• hits HP directly

This allows it to destroy frontline heroes much faster

Virgo ♍️.text:
## __♍ Virgo Set__

__Set Bonus__

2 pieces
• Heal Effect +18%
This increases all healing done by the hero.
4 pieces — Skill: Spica

When the hero uses a healing skill:

the target also gains a shield.

Shield value:
• 20% of the healing amount

Duration:
• 5 seconds

__How It Actually Works__

Every heal becomes:

heal + shield

__Best Heroes for This Set__

This set is perfect for:

Pure healers or Supports that heal the team

__Ideal for__
• AoE healers
• Defensive supports
• Sustain teams

__Not Useful For__

• DPS
• Tanks without healing
• Controllers

because the effect only triggers when healing.

__Interesting Note__

Many competitive teams use Virgo because:
• the shield stacks with healing
• it can save a hero that would otherwise die immediately.

It is one of the best sets for sustain teams, but only need one set in a lineup, even when you are running 2 pure healers comp.

Libra ♎️.text:
## __♎ Libra Set__

__Set Bonus__

2 pieces
• HP +12%

This already makes the hero tankier by default.

4 pieces — Skill: Beta Librae

When the character suffers a control effect, they recover:

6% of their maximum HP

Cooldown:
• 5 seconds

Control effects that trigger the effect:
• Stun
• Immobilize
• Taunt
• Entanglement
• Silence
• Deterrence
• Blockade

__How It Actually Works__

Every time your hero is controlled:

they automatically heal.

__Real Function of the Set__

This set is meant to:

make control-heavy teams much less effective.

If the enemy tries to:
• stun you
• silence you
• immobilize you

you recover HP instead of simply dying.

It is a set designed for anti-CC meta.

__Best Heroes for This Set__

Perfect for:

Frontline tanks
Bruisers
Heroes that stay on the frontline

because they are the ones receiving the most control effects.

__Ideal for__
• Main tanks
• Off-tanks
• Frontline fighters
• support DoT 
• Support Heavy Buffer 
• And Support Heavy Crowd Control

__Not Useful For__

• Backline DPS
• Healers

Scorpio ♏️.text:
## __♏ Scorpio Set__

__Set Bonus__

2 pieces
• HP +12%

This bonus increases the hero’s survivability, which is important because the set activates when the hero takes damage.

4 pieces — Skill: Antares

When the hero takes damage:

Automatically deals a counterattack to the attacker.

Damage dealt

6% of your hero’s Max HP

Important details:
• Ignores enemy DEF
• 5 second cooldown


__How it actually works__

This set converts your hero’s HP into reflected damage.

Example:

If your hero has 1,000,000 HP

when they take damage they deal:

60,000 damage

to the enemy that attacked them.

This damage ignores the enemy’s DEF.

__Real function of the set__

This set punishes enemies that deal frequent damage, especially AoE attacks.

Heroes equipped with Scorpio can deal automatic damage while being attacked, even if they are not offensive characters.

__Best hero type__

Support heroes
Backline sustain heroes

__Why?__

Support heroes are frequently hit by AoE damage during fights.
Scorpio allows them to deal automatic damage whenever they take hits, adding extra damage without needing offensive abilities.

__Not ideal for__

Pure DPS
Frontline tanks (Subject to your build) 

These heroes usually benefit more from sets that improve damage or survivability directly.

Sagittarius ♐️.text:
## __♐ Sagittarius Set__

Set Bonus

2 pieces
• ATK +12%

This alone is already very good because it directly increases all of the hero’s damage.

4 pieces — Skill: Constellar Kaus

When the battle begins:

ATK +30%

Duration:
• 30 seconds

Very important characteristic:

Cannot be dispelled.

This means no enemy can remove this buff.

__How It Actually Works__

At the start of the fight your DPS receives:
• +12% ATK (set bonus)
• +30% ATK (effect)

Total at the start:

+42% ATK

for 30 seconds.

In many games of this type, battles last less than 30 seconds.

__Real Function of the Set__

This is a set designed for:

early burst damage

It is used to:
• eliminate enemies quickly
• win short fights
• deal a lot of damage in the first seconds.

__Best Heroes for This Set__

Perfect for:

• Main DPS
• Burst assassins
• Heroes that deal damage immediately

Ideal for:
• Main carries
• Heroes with devastating ultimates
• Heroes that need to eliminate the enemy backline quickly

__Not Useful For__

• Healers
• Tanks
• Supports

because they do not benefit from the ATK increase.

__Important Note__

Many top players use Sagittarius because:

the buff cannot be dispelled.

So even against teams full of debuffs, the DPS remains boosted.

Capricorn ♑️.text:
## __♑ Capricorn Set__

__Set Bonus__

2 pieces
• CRIT Rate +6%

This increases the chance of landing critical hits.

4 pieces — Skill: Delta Capricorni

Deals:

+13.5% damage

against two specific classes:
• Warriors
• Supports

__How It Actually Works__

The bonus only applies when attacking those classes.

So:
• Against Warriors → +13.5% damage
• Against Supports → +13.5% damage
• Against other classes → no bonus

__Real Function of the Set__

__This set is used to__

kill supports and off-tanks faster.

In many teams:
• Warriors are the frontline
• Supports keep the team alive

So Capricorn helps break the enemy team structure.

__Best Heroes for This Set__

Perfect for:

• Single-target DPS
• Assassins
• Heroes that eliminate the backline

Ideal for:
• Carries that target supports
• Assassins that jump to the backline
• DPS that need to eliminate healers

__Not Useful For__

• Tanks
• Healers
• Supports

because the bonus is designed to increase offensive damage.

Aquarius ♒️.text:
## __♒ Aquarius Set__

__Set Bonus__

2 pieces
• Heal Effect +9%

4 pieces
• Heal Effect +13.5% total

__How It Actually Works__

All healing done by the hero is increased.

__Real Function of the Set__

This set is used to:

maximize pure healing.

It does not provide shields.
It does not provide special effects.

It simply increases healing.
Mainly people use for early game as is easier to obtain. 
Can be combine with virgo 2piece+2piece for a higher healing effect bonus. 

__Best Heroes for This Set__

Perfect for:

Pure healers

Heroes that provide:
• AoE healing
• Continuous healing
• Very strong single-target healing.

Pisces ♓️.text:
## __♓ Pisces Set__

__Set Bonus__

2 pieces
• CRIT Rate +6%

This increases the chance for attacks to land critical hits.

4 pieces — Skill: Eta Piscium

Deals:

+13.5% damage

against two specific classes:
• Mages
• Assassins

__How it actually works__

The bonus only applies against those classes.

Mage → +13.5% DMG
Assassin → +13.5% DMG
Other classes → No bonus

__Real function of the set__

Pisces is designed to eliminate enemy DPS faster.

Usually in most teams:
• Mage = main DPS
• Assassin = burst DPS

Because of this, the set is built to neutralize offensive threats quickly.

__Best hero type__

Single-target DPS
Assassin hunters
Carry heroes focused on eliminating enemy damage dealers

__Ideal for__

• Heroes that attack the backline
• DPS that need to eliminate fragile mages
• Teams that want to remove the main damage dealer early

In PvP this set can be very strong because most teams include mages or assassins.

__Common strategy__

If the meta is full of powerful mages, some DPS players equip Pisces to eliminate them before they can cast their skills.

Omotesando.text:
__Omotesando Guide__

__What is Omotesando__

Omotesando is a progression mode where all heroes are affected by a Curse that greatly reduces their attributes.
To compensate for this penalty, players receive Soul King Blessings, which increase hero stats while progressing.
These effects are only active inside Omotesando.

__Basic Rules__

__Reduced Attributes__

All heroes have their attributes heavily reduced due to the Curse.
As you progress, Soul King Blessings increase your stats and allow you to climb higher ranks.

__Rank System__

Players are divided into different Omotesando ranks depending on their strength.

Higher ranks provide:

• better progression
• more AFK rewards

__AFK Rewards__

The higher your rank, the more Twisted Energy you gain from AFK rewards.
This resource is important for progressing in Omotesando.


__Actions Limit__

Each session allows a maximum of:

200 actions

Actions are consumed when progressing through battles.

__Progression__

__Guards__

To advance in Omotesando you must defeat guards.
Each guard you defeat allows you to move forward in the progression path.

__Mirror Battles__

Your Mirror Image will challenge other players.

If you lose:
the incident disappears.

If you win:
you receive rewards.

__Blessings__

You can receive up to: 6 Blessings per day from other players.
If you do not receive Blessings for a while, your heroes will automatically bless you.
Blessings increase your stats and help you progress further.

__Weekly Reset__

Omotesando resets every week.

__Auto Progress__

Once you reach:

Level 80

and defeat:

80 guards

you unlock the Auto-Progress feature, which allows automatic progression.

__Action Priority__

When choosing which action to take, it is recommended to follow this order:

Love Letter → Chest → Hourglass → Mirror → Prayer → Swords

Zero Squad Palace.text:
## __Zero Squad Palace Guide__

__Overview__

Zero Squad Palace is a challenge mode where players fight against the Guardians of the Palace.
Each week, the strongest players become Guardians and defend their positions while other players try to defeat them and take their place.

__Guardians__

These players will defend their positions inside the Zero Squad Palace.

__Guardian Clones__

The Guardians send clones to test the strength of other players.
The difficulty of these challenges depends on:

• assessment level
• star rating

Higher difficulty means stronger enemies.

__Defense Lineup__

The Guardian’s defense team automatically uses their Arena Defense Lineup.
This means the team you set for Arena defense will also be used here.

__Challenge Attempts__

Players can attempt the challenge unlimited times until they complete the 5-Star difficulty challenge.
This allows players to retry battles and adjust their strategy.

__Challenging the Guardian__

After completing the 5-Star challenge, players can challenge the current Guardian.
If you defeat the Guardian, you become the new Guardian.

__Guardian Title__

Players who become Guardians of the Zero Squad Palace receive the Guardian title for the current session.

__Guardian Ranking Rules__

When challenging a Guardian:

• If you are already a Guardian and your ranking is higher than the Guardian you challenge, both Guardian positions remain unchanged.

• If your ranking is lower and you win the challenge, you swap positions with the Guardian you defeated.

DPS- Assassin & Mage.text:
**DPS - Assassin & Mage Soul Jade Tier List**
Assassin & Mage are mainly running either Shapeless + Taurus(Gold Cloth) or Enrage + Sagittarius(Gold Cloth). The common soul that they share are Swift, Strength & Penetration.

Silence & Armor Breaker are mainly use for debuffing. 

Blade mainly use for unit that ULT a lot like Sukuna, Gojo, etc.

Crit is a debatable soul as if your running a high crit unit with Crimson NP you might want to use Crit Soul, but overall if your crit is about 50% without Crit Soul I do say you can bring another soul that buffs dmg, as you will still crit eventually, it really depends on what you need.

Penetration & Precision, both soul jade are good for dps, overall penetration is used more than precision due to penetration provide better value in general, precision will have more value if your fighting someone with dodge soul jade on all their support+warrior unit.

Starting from Tier B to C, the soul are mainly your sub option, testing different build + soul, not really recommend for main lineup unless your cooking a new comp with it.

**DPS - Assassin & Mage Soul Jade Tier List**
> **S:** Shapeless, Strength, Swift, Enrage, Silence, Penetration
> **A:** Blade, Crit, Armor Breaker, Dodge, Precision
> **B:** Tenacity, Lifesteal, Slow, Cruelty
> **C:** Divine Body, Shield, Purification, Cure, Guardian, Restoration, Divine Blessing, First Aid, DMG Reduction, DMG Reflection, Regeneration, Resilience, Dispel, Devotion, Inspiration, Healing Reduction

Warrior Tierlist.text:
**Warrior Soul Jade Tier List**
**DPS/Half Tank**
Shapeless, Strength, Swift & Tenacity is a common build use for a half tank warrior. You can swap in Penetration/Precision/Silence/Armor Breaker depending on your needs. As mention being a half tank the combo going to be Shapeless + Taurus(Gold Cloth) or if your running a Rage Debuffer would go with Gemini(Gold Cloth) + debuffing soul jade like Silence/Armor Breaker.

**Full Tank**
Tenacity, Swift, Dodge & Shapeless is a common full tank build for warrior. For Shapeless, you could change it to DMG Reflection/Shield/DMG Reduction depending on your need.

Silence & Armor Breaker are both good debuffing soul jade.

Shield & DMG Reflection are good only if your HP is highest, the higher the better outcome. DO NOTE on Shield can't be stack so only need 1 unit in team to use it.

Starting from Tier B to C, the soul are mainly your sub option, testing different build + soul, not really recommend for main lineup unless your cooking a new comp with it.

**Warrior Soul Jade Tier List**
> **S:** Shapeless, Silence, Tenacity, Swift, Dodge, Strength
> **A:** Shield, Armor Breaker, DMG Reduction, Penetration, Precision, DMG Reflection
> **B:** Lifesteal, Crit, Divine Body, Enrage, Blade, Guardian, Dispel, Regeneration, Restoration, Slow, Divine Blessing, First Aid, Healing Reduction
> **C:** Purification, Cure, Cruelty, Resilience, Devotion, Inspiration

Support Tierlist.text:
**PVE Soul Jade Tier List**
Swift on all unit is essential.

Armor Breaker should be equip on 1/2 unit getting the defense break helps boost DMG in PVE.

Penetration, Shapeless & Strength is your main soul for your DPS. Shapeless is best on your DPS as it give extra dmg after 20s.

Tenacity, Dodge & Shield are mainly used on your debuffer/support to keep them alive. Cure is mainly for support for the healing to sustain your team in not dying.

Blade, Enrage & Crit just not a very great Soul against PVE boss.

**Support Soul Jade Tier List**
> **S:** Purification, Cure, Swift, Tenacity, Dodge 
> **A:** Shield, DMG Reflection, Silence, Guardian
> **B:** Shapeless, Armor Breaker, Slow, Divine Body, Dispel, Restoration, Divine Blessing, First Aid, DMG Reduction, Regeneration
> **C:** Lifesteal, Enrage, Strength, Crit, Blade, Precision, Penetration, Cruelty, Resilience, Devotion, Inspiration, Healing Reduction

PvE Tierlist.text:
**PVE Soul Jade Tier List**
Swift on all unit is essential.

Armor Breaker should be equip on 1/2 unit getting the defense break helps boost DMG in PVE.

Penetration, Shapeless & Strength is your main soul for your DPS. Shapeless is best on your DPS as it give extra dmg after 20s.

Tenacity, Dodge & Shield are mainly used on your debuffer/support to keep them alive. Cure is mainly for support for the healing to sustain your team in not dying.

Blade, Enrage & Crit just not a very great Soul against PVE boss.

**PVE Soul Jade Tier List**
> **S:** Swift, Armor Breaker
> **A:** Penetration, Shapeless, Strength
> **B:** Tenacity, Dodge, Shield, Cure
> **C:** Blade, Enrage, Crit

Summon & Hero Assembly.text:
**Advance Summons**
It is recommended to save your summon ticket for the Hero Assembly Event especially for early game F2P players.

**Specific Summons**
It is recommended to save you special card for hero assembly event.

The Special Point you get can be use to exchange new LTD unit upon their first release.

Future Summon Point Shop might update LTD unit like Gilgamesh, Rem & Saber into it. So if you don't plan on using on random light/dark unit is best to save for new LTD or LTD unit being added to it.

The point card draw you can choose a fraction and summon it with 1k/2k points accumulated when using your special ticket to get a random 5star unit.

**Hero Assembly**
You will start seeing Hero Assembly Event on Day 31 of your server age. Main place to get extra resources.

Event on going for 30 days, and it refreshed monthly.

There will be up to 3 rounds for you to complete monthly. Each round takes 260 heroes to complete. Do take note merging 4 star units to 5 star units doesn't count.

**Hero Conversion**
People mainly use this feature if they are lacking the specific copy to star up a unit.

Do note it is possible to get Shining unit from refreshing but the odds of getting them are low and RNG.

Common Treasure Hunt , Advance Treasure.text:
**Common Treasure Hunt**
Normally you would just hit sail 15 times until you get the grand prize and refresh for the next grand prize.

**Advanced Treasure Hunt**
For Advanced Treasure Hunt early game player would need to save at least sail 10 times before you start refreshing for Madoka. But is recommended that you save a lot more Sail before you refresh for Madoka since there's a 70 pull pity for Grand Prize.

This is the easiest way for you to get a strong healer Madoka, which can be used until the end of the game.

For mid/late game player when your Madoka is done building, you can refresh for DIO/Legendary Soul.

Commission.text:
**Commission**
For commission you would always want to refresh and accept quests that give diamonds and special cards.

Refresh for epic, legendary, mythic & eternal diamond commissions.

Karin Tower.text:
**Karin Tower**
Your top 5 highest level character will be automatically added into Karin Tower. Among the top 5 units, your lowest level character will be the level of your Karin Tower.

Units that are being placed into the slot in Karin Tower will share the level of your Karin Tower.

**Holy Water Refining**
The Holy Water Refining is a late game stats boost where you convert your EXP Candies into stats. The higher your level for leveling up your refining, the more EXP Candies is needed to convert into Holy Water.

Wish & Development.text:
**Wish**
Wish is the main place where you will be getting pets, you can choose to summon blue dragon ball. But I recommend that you buy blue dragon ball and merge it into purple dragon ball to summon.

Obviously that orange dragon ball is exclusive and rare to get as there's a pity for you to get a legendary pet upon using certain amount of orange dragon ball.

**Development**
This is where you will level up your divine dragon for extra stats, the higher your stats the stronger your pet will be.

Summon & Companion Assembly.text:
**Summon & Companion Assembly**
Companion Assembly will arrive when your server age Day 70.

The summon is simple you choose an awakened pet in the summon pool to increase its drop rate while you do summon. Do note you still can get another awakened pet while you perform a summon, just the one you choose has a higher % in getting it.

The Companion Summon is pretty much the same as Hero Assembly, is a monthly event which has 3 rounds each round and needs 500 summon to complete.

Do save your summon, making sure you can get 500 summon for 1 full round reward. The rewards are good to have for the long run.

Archive & Star Chart.text:
**Archive**
For archive pets, this will be the spot where all the CP comes from, getting and unlocking different pets for Bonds in the archive give a tremendous amount of CP.

Once you're done in getting your Blue and Purple pets to 5 stars, you can choose to salvage them as they won't be much use for them. But I recommend keeping  a 5 star Yui & Luna, as they have some usage.

As for the legendary pets non awaken & awaken, you can get multiple 5 stars copy of them, as they will come in handy for 3 team comp.

**Star Chart**
There will be 3 pages of Star Chart

Each page has 3 different skills. In page 1 each skill needs 3 Spiritual Crystalization (Pet Crystal) to unlock. Page 2 6 crystal & Page 3 9 crystal.

Among the 3 skills in each page, skill 1 and 2 are stats skills, meaning you will get extra stats for your team. Whereas Skill 3 is an actual skill. You would need to prioritize getting skill 3 on each page, as 1 extra skill during pvp helps a lot. Same goes to page 2 & 3.

Blessing.text:
**Blessing**
There are 4 classes of Blessing, Warrior, Assassin, Mage, Support

Is advisable in getting one of the main DPS class Assassin/Mage to full red blessing in prior as it will help boost your dmg by a lot.

The other 3 class blessings you can get to full orange, as getting 1 full red blessing will be a long journey for F2P/mid spender.

As for whale focus on 1 of the DPS your main at, and start getting warrior and support to full red blessing.

Pet Order & Awaken Skill.text:
**Pet Order & Awaken Skills**
Awaken Pets, are pets that have extra awakening skills. Currently there are only 5 pets with awaken skill and 5 of them can be split into 2 groups 

Group 1 are Happy and Kyubey where their awakened skills need 16 flames to activate.

Group 2 are Soul, Chopper and Slug, where their awakened skills need 12 flames to activate starting from 3 stars.

Knowing that information, pets have a cool down of 30 seconds after they use their skill whether the skill is awakened or normal.

Iggy and kanta are both strong openers who give flames when using their skill, what do the flames do? Those flames are like your mana pool, awaken skill like souls need 12 mana to activate or even their normal skill takes 8 mana to activate.

Knowing that we can place our pet order by using Iggy or kanta as our openers. Since Iggy also lowers defense and gives flames, most people would choose Iggy to be their opener. Choosing Kanta as opener you will also get flames from it, which is extremely close in getting 12 flames at the start of the battler after your Kanta uses his skills.

Kanta uses his skills, there's a small cooldown before the next pet can use his skill, within the small cooldown, you can accumulate to 12 flames which is enough for a soul/chopper/slug to use their awaken skill.

Iggy uses his skills but you won't have enough for 12 flame to use your awaken skill right off the battle, but worry not Kanta being placed at the 4th rotating back to 2nd set of skill you will have enough flames by the time your awakened pet uses their skill the 2nd time.

If you want to use Kanta as openers for the 12 flame, Kanta needs to be a 3 stars same as your Soul/Chopper/Slug they also needs to be at 3 stars.

Orientation:
1) Iggy + awaken + awaken + Kanta
2) Kanta + awaken + awaken + Iggy

Details & Awakening.text:
**Goddess Details**
All goddesses share the same level and goddess sets that you have. So when you're leveling up your goddess it levels up for all.

The goddess weapon sets are capped at 3 stars.

**Goddess Awakening**
For the Goddess Awakening is pretty much the same as leveling up your goddess, they share the same awakening. The higher your awakening the stronger the goddess skills and passive will be.

For a F2P or mid spender who wanted to use their diamond on better stuff, just get 1 set black & white goddess set at 1 star is sufficient. Going up to 3 star won't really change much.

Card Collection.text:
**Card Collection**
Cards are pretty straightforward to understand, you get the card, you level it up for extra stats.

You can further enhance the stats in Duel Disk Enchantment.

As for whether you should save your card pack ticket for an event like spend tickets get extra rewards similar to Hero Assembly or Companion Summons, unfortunately NA server till now still don't have the event for it. TW(Taiwan Server) has an event for it. So whether you wanna save just in case a future event might have it, the choice is up to you

Sigil Engrave & Artifact Mirror.text:
**Artifacts**
Artifacts are pretty straightforward you get sigil you combine it and you get better artifacts unlocks.

**Sigil Engrave & Artifact Mirror**
Sigil Engrave is where you dump all the blue, purple and orange sigil into it to level up to get extra stats.

Artifact Mirror is basically you mirror the current artifact and get an extra duplication of it. It doesn't duplicate the sigil you engrave onto it. It only just duplicates the artifact itself.

For example the millennium puzzle currently I got my millennium puzzle activated. If I want another millennium puzzle for my team 2 unit, I need to mirror it to get another millennium puzzle, and I also need another 6 prismatic sigil to engrave in activating it.

In short, Artifact Mirror is a very very late game thing to do, most people won't even use that feature.

Hogyoku & Sigil Divine (Summon).text:
**Hogyoku**
Hogyoku is basically where you grind your ass on infinite realm boss and get the chest open it hoping to get soul essences.

These soul essences are being used to unlock the Hogyoku skills.

Bare in mind you do not need to unlock it in order from skill 1 to skill 4.

You can straight away unlock skill 2 & 4 which are the most important skills, as it lets each unit wear up to 3 artifacts.

Skill 2 lets your unit wear 2 artifacts in total, and skill 4 lets your unit wear 3 artifacts in total.

Skill 2 cost 2 soul essence to unlock. Skill 4 cost 4 soul essence to unlock.

**Sigil Divine (Summon)**
The main place where u RNG to get your pink sigil to combine into red, eternal and prismatic.

You will get the summon for sigil from either artifact pass or the chest from defeating Infiniti real bosses.

Hook.text:
**Hook**
There's multiple hooks you can buy and each hook has its own effect, hooks effect stacks.

On the mining page as a F2P player your main goal will be hunting special card summon, while hunting for special cards, grab all the star stone on the way. Sometimes when you see some star stone are glowing, those are basically % stats star stone.

Refresh after getting your special card.

The TNT is used to bomb those items you don't want.

The apple is used to speed up by 60 second. A cool feature, if u got 5 or more apple, when u use on item that are more than 5min it will instantly obtain it, for example a red gem might need to wait around 2hour but with 5 apple you can get it instantly.

The macaroon helps u get the item instantly no matter what the time is.

Star Stone.text:
**Star Stone**
For this feature you will be able to reduce the size of your star stone you get from 5 all the way down to 1.

Understanding the basics, when we mine for star stone, our stones are basically a 5 block stone.

So how do we reduce the size so we could put more stone on screen?

If you look closely there's a star stone bag feature, you'll tap on tap and it will open up your bag.

In your bag there are two features, which are Smelt and Combine.

You will first click combine, in the combine page you can click auto craft, after crafting it, go back to the smelt and combine page.

Click on Smelt feature, on screen there's a quick melt feature upon clicking it it will show you 4 options to check 5-slot star stone, 4,3 & 2.

We will start by clicking 5-slot star stone, checking it and clicking yes, it will automatically help you smelt those 5-slot into 4-slot.

After that, go back to combine, and click auto craft, you should be getting some extra new 4 star combine if you have a lot of star stone saved up.

Repeat the process again, going back to smelt now check for 4-slot star stone and auto melt it, go back to auto combine and repeat the process until you get the smallest 1 block star stone.

Why do we need 1 block star stone? Cause there's a limited amount of space and if we can get the whole page filled with a 1 block prismatic our stats would be super ideal and fats chunks of stats.

Map & Mine Level.text:
**Map**
The more you mine. The better the map you will unlock, prizes like red star stone will have a higher chance of appearing in the map.

**Mine Level**
Mine Levels are easy to understand, you mine for star stone or other rewards, you get extra ore to level up your mine, the higher the level it unlocks extra stats and more slots for placing your star stone.

Fishing.text:
**Fishing**
Fishing is the best way for you to obtain stats.

So how do we maximize fishing?
First off we need to understand that we will be getting 90 breads per fishing day, and our max breads we can save are 180, the fishing order mission says if you fish 200 times, it will automatically help you to complete the quest no matter what progression your on.

Knowing that information, we first have to save 180 days on 2 fishing days, use 30 bread so that you will be on 150 breads before the end of the day.

On the third fishing day of the week, you will first obtain 30 breads, which put you on 180, start refreshing the fishing order to easy quests like fishing for aquatic debris like rock, socks, heart of the ocean and etc. Those easy 3 star fishing orders which don't require you to fish for actual fish, are the most easy to complete.

Do keep an eye out when you complete the order for those debris quests, submit the order and refresh for another easy 1-3 star quest. Upon finishing the 180 breads, we will get another 60 more breads here's where you need to pay attention, fish 19 bread so our total goes to 199, and stop fishing, go to the fish order refresh for those 3-5star order, and fish 1 time and boom it will automatically complete all those hard quest orders so that you can maximize the rewards earn.

Archive & Bait.text:
**Fish Archive & Bait**
Knowing how to maximize our fishing rewards earn, for the archive, the better the section we are in, the better the fish stats are.

Upon upgrading our fish aquarium we can bring more rare fish.

Do take note unlocking newer and harder areas, the fish escape rate or fishing line broke will gradually increase until you upgrade your rods.

Completing the fishing archive gives a fat chunk of stats for your account, this is where you get apart from others if you fish seriously.

Baits, do we need to buy bait or use free bait?
Majority of the time you will be using the free bait, if your hunting for specific aquatic/fish, you can buy the designated fish bait in increasing the odds of fishing it, but is totally RNG.

Gear: Level Up & Refine.text:
**Fishing Gear to Level Up**
Our main focus will be on the fishing line and fishing float.

Our sub focus would be the fishing rod itself.

The line and float are the main factor determine on whether your fish escape or fish line broke.

The fishing rod itself is where you sell fish and sell of the drops for more sea shell to upgrade your aquarium.

**Gear Refine**
If we pay close attention towards upgrading our fishing line/floats/rod, there's a refine option.

In the refine option we would need to hunt for the rare stats vitality, cleaner and aquarist.

The higher your rod upgraded the better the refinement stats you will be getting for those rare stats.

If you're wondering what those stats do please read it in game or the screenshot attached below.

Auction.text:
**Auction**
Auction is a daily event that happens everyday from 12 in game time to 6 pm in game time. There will be 2 section, 12 pm to 3pm is Alliance Auction, where 3 pm to 6pm is world auction.

Things to buy from auction, Awakening Gemstone, Reformation Gear, Reformation Time Wheel & Spiritual Crystallization.

Aside from Awakening Gemstone which will be there daily, the gear, time wheel and pet crystal are considered rare drops in auction, once you see them appear in the auction shop for the day, if possible do buy out, as the longer you wait to bid for it chances of people buying it would increase.

Note only do auction if you have extra diamonds (after spending it on events and actually necessary stuff).

Noble Phantasm.text:
**Noble Phantasm**
There are 2 groups of Noble Phantasm:
1) Reformation Gear - Primeval Flame and Avalon
2) Reformation Time Wheel - All the other Noble Phantasm except for flame & avo.

Which NP to focus on getting?
Flame and Avo are basically an easy must get, as you progress through the game, do auctions for these while you have extra diamonds.

Depending on which DPS your running, most people would first focus on getting Rupture or  Dual Rose Spear for their DPS, as for support focus on getting Excalibur and Rho.

Time Wheel priorities in using it:
1) DPS: Rule breaker/Dual Rose Spear/ Crimson Lotus - as your main DPS using it you need them at full prismatic which cost you 5 time wheels.
2) Rupture - Use 2 time wheel on rupture to get the minus 300 rage ability. If your using for your main DPS, get it to prismatic.
3) Excalibur - Get Excalibur up to prismatic when your done with the above 2 NP.
4) Upgrade those which you will be using either for sub dps or support.

Reformation Gear priority:
Unlike Time Wheel, Gear is considered easy to obtain.
1) Flame get it to prismatic 
2) Avalon get it to prismatic
3) Get both to +5

Auction Dividend.text:
## Auction Dividend by RandomStranger
NA & EU:
- Anyone hit on the alliance world boss (before auction start ofc) will get % commission from auction diamonds spent on Alliance Bidding.
- Max cap is 3000 diamonds from % commission.
- The ideal number is: people hit on bosss should be ≤ 8.
- The ammount of diamond recieve would be: [total diamonds spent on auction] ÷ number of people hitting boss = 3000 diamonds max.
`for example: 3000 max of 8 people = 3000 * 8 = 24.000 diamonds need to be spent in alliance auction`
- Any spare NP, goddess gear, reform gear & timewheel that didn't get bought will be transfer into World Bidding.
- If anyone bid/ buyout the spare items from your alliance, you will get another split of commission (max is still 3000)
- So basically if the server on has less than 3 guilds, you can manipulate to have 6.000 diamonds a day.

Ninja War pt1.text:
**1. What is Ninja War**
Ninja War is a guild event where 48 guilds compete against each other, divided into groups of 12 guilds.
The objective of the event is to capture as many fortresses as possible, including those controlled by rival guilds.
Guilds must fight and compete for control of these fortresses in order to gain an advantage during the event.

**2. Starting the Event**
At the beginning of the event, each player starts at their guild’s base.
From there, players must capture adjacent fortresses in order to move across the map and expand their control.
Some fortresses provide special advantages, making them strategic points that guilds should prioritize capturing and defending.

**3. Ninja War Phases**
The Ninja War event is divided into two phases:
Phase 1 – Fortress Conquest and Defense
Duration: 9 hours
During this phase, guilds focus on capturing and defending fortresses across the map.

Phase 2 – Base Destruction
Duration: 3 hours
During this phase, the shields protecting guild bases are removed, allowing players to attack and destroy enemy guild bases.

**4. How to Win Ninja War**
There are two ways to win the Ninja War event:
1. Victory by Points
The guild that controls the highest number of fortresses will gain the advantage in points.
2. Total Domination
A guild can also win by capturing all fortresses on the map and destroying all enemy guild bases.

Ninja War pt2.text:
**5. Chakra System**
In Ninja War, players do not fight using their hero teams.
Instead, each player’s Combat Power (CP) is converted into Chakra.
The higher the player’s CP, the greater the amount of Chakra they will receive for the event.
Chakra represents the player’s strength during Ninja War and is required to perform actions on the battlefield.

**6. Chakra Consumption and Recovery**
Chakra is the main resource used during Ninja War.
Chakra Consumption
Chakra is consumed in two ways:
1. Capturing Fortresses
This action consumes a smaller amount of Chakra.
2. Fighting Other Players
Battles against other players consume a larger amount of Chakra.

**Chakra Recovery**
During the event, Chakra regenerates over time.
Players can return to their guild base to recharge their Chakra in two ways:
1. Collecting the Chakra generated over time
2. Using Chakra Pills
    •    1 Chakra Pill = 500 Chakra
Chakra Pills can be obtained in several ways:
    •    Purchased in the event shop using points earned from controlling fortresses (Chakara Pills can be saved for concurrent days, expired after event ends)
    •    Purchased using gems
    •    Purchased using real money
The packs that can be bought with event points or gems are limited in quantity.

**7. Player Battles and Merit Points**
Battles between players not only consume Chakra, but also grant Merit Points.
Merit Points are personal points used to determine the individual player ranking at the end of the event.
This ranking is independent of the guild that wins the Ninja War, meaning players can still achieve a high personal ranking regardless of their guild’s final result.
Merit Points are also used to unlock milestone rewards during the event.

**8. Ten Tails Boss**
If a guild manages to capture the entire map, a special boss called Ten Tails will appear.
This boss does not affect the victory conditions of the event.
Its purpose is to allow players to spend their remaining Chakra, since unused Chakra will not carry over to the next day.
Players can use their extra Chakra to attack the Ten Tails before the event ends.

**9. Guild Ranking Rewards**
At the end of the Ninja War event, guilds receive rewards based on their final ranking.
The rewards for the first-ranked guild are divided into three categories depending on the player’s role within the guild.

1. Alliance Leader: The guild leader receives the highest tier of rewards, including exclusive items and titles.

2. Vice Leader / Elite: Deputy leaders and elite members receive high-level rewards, slightly lower than those of the guild leader.

3. Members: All other guild members receive participation rewards, which are lower but still valuable.

Initial Realm pt1.text:
__INITIAL REALM GUIDE__

__Event Objective__

The objective of the event is for the alliance to explore floors, defeat the Realm Guardians, and collect rewards and points.
    •    Floors are explored cooperatively with the alliance
    •    Higher floors provide better rewards
    •    Progression depends on defeating the Realm Guardian of each floor

__Stamina (Main Resource)__

Each exploration consumes Stamina.
    •    Higher floors cost more Stamina (from Floor 11 onward, each exploration costs 2 Stamina instead of 1, increases according to floor level your on)
    •    Stamina recovers over time
    •    Energy Potions can be used to restore Stamina
Moving across already explored tiles does not consume Stamina.

__Exploration__

Players can explore only obstacles connected to their current position.
Each exploration may trigger a random event.
Possible events include:
    •    Points
    •    Buffs
    •    Traps
    •    Monsters
    •    Crystals
Completing events grants points and Base EXP.

__Floor Progression__

The main objective during exploration is to locate and defeat the Realm Guardian of the floor.
    •    Each floor contains one Realm Guardian
    •    Defeating the Realm Guardian is the only way to unlock the next floor
    •    Once defeated, the alliance can advance to the next floor
Exploration should focus on finding the Realm Guardian quickly in order to speed up alliance progression.

__Magic Power__

Magic Power determines the damage dealt during battles.
Magic Power is based on:
    •    The highest power your account has ever reached
    •    Crystals obtained during the event
Crystals are removed when the event ends.

__Combat__

When a monster is discovered:
    •    The Finder receives the kill rewards
    •    If the Finder fails, allies can assist
    •    Assisting players receive assist rewards
The Finder may spend additional Stamina to attack again.

__Assist System__

Helping allies consumes one Assist Attempt.
    •    Assist attempts are limited
    •    Additional attempts can be obtained by spending Stamina
Helping allies defeat stronger monsters allows the alliance to progress faster.

__Alliance Base__

Completing events grants Base EXP.
The EXP collected by all alliance members increases the Alliance Base level.
The Base level determines the number of tokens the alliance receives during the Guild Battle Phase.
Example:
Base Level 5 → 5 Blessing Jade
These Blessing Jades represent the durability of the base.
When the base is attacked and its HP goes from 100% to 0%, the alliance loses 1 Blessing Jade.
Example:
Base Lv.5 → 5 Blessing Jade
Base destroyed once → 4 Blessing Jade remaining
Blessing Jade can also become negative (for example -1, -2, etc.).
Blessing Jade are used only to determine the alliance position on the leaderboard and decide which guilds advance to the next round.
During the Guild Battle Phase, players can continue exploring floors and completing events.

__Treasure Floors__

After certain floors, a Treasure Floor appears.
    •    Each player can open one chest
    •    Higher floors provide better rewards
Unclaimed rewards will not be resent later.

Initial Realm pt2.text:
__Ancient Battlefield__

The Ancient Battlefield opens during the final 3 hours of the event.
During this period, alliances can fight against other guilds.
These battles influence the blessing jade count and alliance ranking.
Base cannot be destroyed as mentioned above base at negative blessing jade count can still attack other base.

__Rankings__

__Personal Ranking__

Based on points earned from events.
Tiebreaker: Magic Power

__Alliance Ranking__

Alliance rankings can be determine by rank placement of the day much similar to how ninja war work.
Based on the sum of round scores.
Tiebreaker: Alliance Power.

__Stamina Milestone Rewards__

The game tracks the total Stamina spent during the event.
Reaching certain thresholds grants additional rewards.

__Alliance Welfare__

Crystals obtained during the event can be shared as alliance rewards.
    •    All members can claim them
    •    These rewards contribute to Guild Badge progression

When the Guild Badge reaches the maximum level, it generates an Energy Potion that all members can claim.

__Event Pass__

The event includes a progression pass with two reward tracks.

__Free Track__

All players can access the free track.
The most valuable reward obtained from the free track is Energy Potions, since Stamina is the main limiting resource during the event.

__Premium Track__

Players can unlock the Premium Pass for 29.99$.

Once the pass is fully completed, the most valuable rewards include:
    •    20 copies of the Legendary Card
    •    50 Collector’s Packs

Final Battle Island pt1.text:
## __**ISLAND OF FINAL BATTLE — FULL MECHANICS GUIDE **__

__Participation Eligibility__

Battle Island is organized by alliances.
You must join an alliance to participate.
After the announcement phase ends:

• the list of participating alliances is locked
• alliance positions are also locked

⚠️ Members can still leave or switch alliances, but:
the event will still use the original locked alliance list
Only alliances that meet the required power threshold can participate.
The event is divided into 4 phases:
• Announcement
• Preparation
• Battle
• Reward

Final Battle Island pt2.text:
__**MATCHING**__

__During the announcement phase__
    
• alliances are grouped based on power
• all alliances start in the Beginner Battlefield
    
__Each round__
    
• alliances with the highest damage in their group advance
• others remain in their current position

Detailed rankings can be viewed in the event interface
    
__In later rounds__
    
• alliances are reshuffled
• matchmaking is based on:
• current alliance points
• if tied → total damage
    
__Alliance damage is determined by__
    
• total damage dealt
• total HP of all members

⚠️ When an alliance island HP reaches 0:
any further damage will NOT be counted

Final Battle Island pt3.text:
__**CONSTRUCTION**__

__Construction includes__
    
• Gold Donations
• Item Donations

__Gold Donations__
    
• consume gold
• have a limit per round
• once the limit is reached → no more gold donations allowed

__Item Donations__
    
• no limit
• require specific items
    
__Each donation__

• grants construction rewards
• grants alliance EXP

__When requirements are met__

- the alliance levels up
- island HP increases

__Some construction levels__
    
• increase alliance star rating
• this is only a visual indicator

__All changes__
    
• construction
• power
⚠️ take effect in the next round
